```lean
theorem perm_eraseIdx {l1 l2 : List α} {hPerm : l1.Perm l2}
  {idx1 : Fin l1.length} {idx2 : Fin l2.length} :
  l1[idx1] = l2[idx2] → (l1.eraseIdx idx1).Perm (l2.eraseIdx idx2)
  := by
  intro h
  have h' := hPerm.erase_idx (idx1 : Nat)
  have h'' : l2.length ≥ 1 := by
    by_contra h₁
    have h₂ : l2 = [] := by
      have h₃ : l2.length = 0 := by omega
      have h₄ : l2 = [] := by
        cases l2 with
        | nil => rfl
        | cons _ _ => simp_all
      exact h₄
    have h₃ : l1 = [] := by
      have h₄ : l1.Perm [] := by simpa [h₂] using hPerm
      have h₅ : l1 = [] := by
        cases l1 with
        | nil => rfl
        | cons _ _ =>
          have h₆ := h₄
          simp [List.Perm] at h₆
          <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
      exact h₅
    have h₄ : (idx1 : ℕ) < l1.length := Fin.is_lt idx1
    simp_all
  -- We need to find an index to erase in l2 to match the erasure in l1.
  have h''' : ∃ z : ℕ, z < l2.length ∧ l2.get ⟨z, by omega⟩ = l1.get ⟨(idx1 : ℕ), by
    omega⟩ := by
    use (idx2 : ℕ)
    <;> simp_all [List.get]
    <;>
    (try omega) <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
    (try aesop)
  obtain ⟨z, hz, hz'⟩ := h'''
  have h'''' := hPerm.erase_idx z
  -- Now we can use the fact that the permutations match to get the desired result.
  have h''''' : (l1.eraseIdx (idx1 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := by
    have h₁ : (l1.eraseIdx (idx1 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := by
      -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
      have h₂ := h'''' |>.trans (List.erase_perm (l2 := (l1))) <;>
        simp_all [List.Perm] <;>
        aesop
    exact h₁
  -- Convert the erasure in l2 to the given index
  have h'''''' : (l2.eraseIdx z) = (l2.eraseIdx z) := rfl
  have h''''''' : (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)) = (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)) := rfl
  -- Use the fact that the permutations match to get the desired result.
  have h'''''''' : (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := by
    -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
    have h₁ : (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := by
      -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
      have h₂ := hPerm
      -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
      have h₃ : (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)) = (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)) := rfl
      have h₄ : (l2.eraseIdx z) = (l2.eraseIdx z) := rfl
      -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
      have h₅ : (l2.get ⟨(↑idx2 : ℕ), by
        have h₆ : (↑idx2 : ℕ) < l2.length := Fin.is_lt idx2
        omega
        ⟩) = (l1.get ⟨(↑idx1 : ℕ), by
        have h₆ : (↑idx1 : ℕ) < l1.length := Fin.is_lt idx1
        omega
        ⟩) := by
        simp_all [List.get]
        <;> aesop
      -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
      have h₆ : (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)) = (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)) := rfl
      have h₇ : (l2.eraseIdx z) = (l2.eraseIdx z) := rfl
      -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
      have h₈ : (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := by
        -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
        have h₉ := hPerm
        -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
        simp_all [List.Perm, List.erasePerm]
        <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
        (try aesop)
      exact h₈
    exact h₁
  -- Combine the results to get the final permutation
  have h''''''' : (l1.eraseIdx (↑idx1 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)) := by
    have h₁ : (l1.eraseIdx (↑idx1 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := h'''''
    have h₂ : (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := h''''''''
    have h₃ : (l1.eraseIdx (↑idx1 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)) := by
      -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
      have h₄ : (l2.eraseIdx (↑idx2 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := h''''''''
      have h₅ : (l1.eraseIdx (↑idx1 : ℕ)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx z) := h'''''
      -- Use the fact that the permutations match to find the right indices
      exact h₄.symm.trans h₅
    exact h₃
  -- Convert back to Fin indices
  simpa [List.eraseIdx] using h'''''''
